claims.text: Fraud detection systems that rely only on human behavioral invariants have systematic blind spots under the A2A scenarios tested here.
theorem_assumptions: The detector family relies only on human behavioral invariants
The evaluated scenarios are the A2A cases described in the manuscript
Agent-native signals are not included in the baseline detector family

claims.text: The five-signal framework measures agent-native economic rationality, network topology, value flow, temporal consistency, and cross-platform correlation, with CP inactive in the current single-chain deployment.
Synthetic validation reaches F1 = 88.71% and ROC-AUC = 0.97 on 100,000 synthetic addresses.
Real-data transfer is partial: recall is 61.4% at the fixed synthetic threshold and 95.4% only after threshold re-optimization, while cleaned-label composite ROC-AUC is 0.515.
Attack-injection validation detects 7 of 8 injected attack chains at 100% recall, while Chain 7 exposes a population-level detection gap for per-address scoring.

The practical contribution is a bounded research and implementation agenda: deploy low-regret P0/P1 overlays, build population-level detection, collect cleaner labels, activate multi-chain correlation, and treat policy recommendations as hypotheses.
